channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: US Enforcement Actions Against Venezuelan Oil Shipments Impact Cuba's Fuel Supply
item.description: According to the Sky News article, the United States under President Donald Trump has implemented measures to intercept and disrupt Venezuelan oil shipments destined for Cuba. At the beginning of 2026...

item.title: Prince remembered by friends and collaborators a decade after his death
item.description: Ten years after Prince’s death, friends, collaborators and family members reflect on the life, talent and complexity of one of music’s most distinctive artists. George Clinton recalls recognising Prin...

item.title: President Samia Directs Ocean Road Cancer Institute to Maintain Advanced Cancer Diagnostic Equipment
item.description: President Samia Suluhu Hassan has given clear instructions to the management of the Ocean Road Cancer Institute (ORCI) in Dar es Salaam to ensure proper and regular maintenance of modern diagnostic ma...

item.title: Security Forces Heighten Alert in BARMM Ahead of First Parliamentary Elections in September
item.description: The Philippine government is strengthening security measures across the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM) as preparations intensify for the region's first parliamentary elections...

item.title: Anthony Olanrewaju Recognised for Impactful Leadership in Nigeria’s Tech Scene
item.description: Anthony Olanrewaju Aderinwale, wey be co-founder and CEO of Hermplify Technologies Ltd., don make Nigeria Top 100 Persons of the Year list by 40Under40 Nigeria. Dis recognition dey celebrate people we...

item.title: Pierre Poilievre’s attacks on Mark Carney reveal deeper issues for the Conservative Party
item.description: In this opinion piece, National affairs columnist Gary Mason argues that the federal Conservative Party’s primary challenge is not the new Liberal majority government under Prime Minister Mark Carney,...

item.title: NSW Police disperse protesters at demonstration against Israeli President Isaac Herzog's visit to Sydney
item.description: On 17 April 2026, New South Wales Police dispersed crowds of protesters in Sydney during a demonstration related to Israeli President Isaac Herzog's visit to Australia. Footage captured by the Sydney ...

item.title: Keir Starmer faces growing pressure over Peter Mandelson security vetting failure
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer has come under intense pressure following revelations that Peter Mandelson was appointed as US ambassador despite failing security vetting. Starmer insisted he was not info...

item.title: Bushra Bibi returns to Adiala Jail after successful eye surgery in Rawalpindi hospital
item.description: Bushra Bibi, wife of former Prime Minister Imran Khan, was shifted back to Adiala Jail in Rawalpindi on Friday after undergoing eye surgery at a private hospital. According to jail officials, she had ...

item.title: Bellarmine Mugabe and Co-accused Return to Court Over Hyde Park Shooting
item.description: Bellarmine Mugabe, son of the late Zimbabwean president Robert Mugabe, is back in court today, April 17, 2026, along with his co-accused, Tobias Matonhodze. The two are facing charges of attempted mur...

item.title: Weekly Highlights from Political Cartoonists Across the U.S.
item.description: Every week, political cartoonists from across the United States contribute their satirical perspectives on current political events. These artists, representing a broad range of political viewpoints, ...

item.title: Three individuals arrested in Quezon City for hospital fraud scheme
item.description: Three individuals were arrested in Quezon City on April 17, 2026, following the exposure of an attempt to defraud a local hospital. The suspects were allegedly involved in falsifying documents to clai...

item.title: Commonwealth Secretary General reaffirms support for Tanzania's political reforms and national dialogue
item.description: The Commonwealth has strongly reaffirmed its full support for Tanzania's ongoing efforts to build a stable, inclusive, and credible political environment. This came through a message from Commonwealth...

item.title: Parliament Introduces Bill to Temporarily Cut VAT on Fuel from 16% to 8%
item.description: In Nairobi, the National Assembly has started the process of looking at a new Bill that aims to reduce the cost of fuel for Kenyans. Majority Leader Kimani Ichungwah introduced the Value Added Tax Ame...

item.title: INEC Chairman Joash Amupitan to Deliver Keynote at Stakeholders Policy Dialogue on Electoral Reforms
item.description: The Chairman of the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC), Professor Joash Amupitan, go deliver the keynote address at a high-level policy dialogue wey dey focus on electoral reforms for Ni...

item.title: Pakistan and Saudi Arabia extend $3 billion deposit to support external reserves
item.description: Pakistan and Saudi Arabia signed an agreement on Friday to extend the maturity of a $3 billion deposit placed by the Saudi Fund for Development with the State Bank of Pakistan. The signing ceremony to...

item.title: Apple's Extensive R&D Presence and Acquisitions in Israel's Tech Sector
item.description: Apple maintains deep technological connections with Israel, highlighted by its second-largest research and development center worldwide in Tel Aviv, employing around 2,500 people. This facility has co...

item.title: Government urged to ban resale of concert tickets above face value
item.description: Fans and industry figures have called on the UK government to introduce legislation preventing the resale of concert tickets above their original face value. Reports indicate that many music enthusias...

item.title: Canterbury University Study Finds Vaping Chemicals Likely Carcinogenic Based on Animal Data and Chemical Analysis
item.description: A new study from the University of Canterbury, published in the New Zealand Medical Journal, analyzed the chemical composition of vape aerosol and assessed its potential cancer risk by comparing it wi...

item.title: Meghan Trainor clarifies limited role in social group controversy and expresses concern for others
item.description: Meghan Trainor has addressed the recent controversy surrounding a celebrity social circle, often referred to as a “toxic mom group,” following an essay by Ashley Tisdale. Tisdale described her negativ...
